ehr.primaryDx: Generalized anxiety disorder with major depressive episode, moderate

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Walk me through what happened that brought you in.

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: I was feeling really hopeless for a few days. I thought about not wanting to be here, but I never had a plan or anything specific.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: When you say not wanting to be here, can you tell me more about those thoughts?

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: Just passive. Like, it would be easier to not exist. Not something I would act on. I want to be around for my kids.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Are you sleeping, eating, managing at home?

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: Sleep has been hard. But I'm getting up, getting the kids to school, doing the laundry. My wife has been really supportive.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Tell me about your home situation.

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: My wife and I have been married twelve years. She knows what I'm going through and she's been amazing through all of this.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: We're going to admit you for stabilization and to make sure you're safe.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He is calm and cooperative. No acute behavioral concerns noted on initial assessment.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: We'll keep his current medications the same for now and monitor over the next few days.

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: I think I just needed a break and some support to get back on track. Being here feels safe.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Day five. Let's see where he is.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He's been sleeping about six hours a night, up from four at admission. Eating all meals, no prompting needed.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He's been attending all groups and engaging well with the milieu. Staff have not had any behavioral concerns.

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: I feel a lot better. The passive thoughts are mostly gone. I'm thinking about getting back to my routine.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Any thoughts of hurting yourself at this point?

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: No. I'm future-oriented. I want to get home to my family and start outpatient therapy. I have things to look forward to.

segments.speaker: social_worker
segments.text: His wife has been present for visiting hours every day and they have been working through a safety plan together.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Medications are unchanged and he is stable on the current regimen.

segments.speaker: social_worker
segments.text: We've scheduled an outpatient therapist appointment for next week and his prescriber follow-up is confirmed.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: He is making good progress. Let's plan for discharge in the next day or two pending continued stability.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He has been oriented and coherent throughout. No evidence of disorganized thinking or behavioral dyscontrol.

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: I'm ready to go home. I feel like I have the tools I need and my wife and I have talked through the plan.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Day seven. How is he doing?

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: Continued stability. He slept well, ate all meals independently, participated in morning group without issues.

segments.speaker: patient
segments.text: I feel like myself again. I'm not having any dark thoughts. I just want to get back to my kids.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: The safety plan is in place and he's endorsed it with his wife. Outpatient is arranged.

segments.speaker: social_worker
segments.text: Discharge paperwork is ready. His wife will pick him up this afternoon and he knows to call the crisis line or come to the ED if symptoms worsen.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: We have not discussed partial hospitalization or IOP as alternatives during this admission. He went directly to inpatient and is now going directly to outpatient.

segments.speaker: psychiatrist
segments.text: Medications remain unchanged throughout the stay. No titration was needed.

segments.speaker: nurse
segments.text: He is managing all activities of daily living independently and has been since day three of the stay.
